meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Escape Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop building in circles. Learn how to implement actionable analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to validate your SaaS MVP and scale revenue faster.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start measuring. By integrating event-based analytics directly into your MVP using Antigravity and Claude Code, you gain immediate visibility into user behavior. This data-driven feedback loop transforms passive coding into active product validation, ensuring every feature you ship actually drives revenue.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks chasing the perfect boilerplate, watching endless YouTube tutorials, and tweaking CSS until your eyes glaze over. Yet, your production dashboard remains a ghost town. You are trapped in the 'tutorial hell' cycle—building for yourself rather than for a market that pays. The difference between a hobbyist and a founder is the feedback loop. You aren't just writing code; you are building a commercial engine.

To break free, you must instrument your application from day one. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent implementation, you can ship an MVP that reports exactly where users drop off. Stop guessing what your users want and start building what the data demands.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the single event that represents user success. This is your foundation for tracking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current SaaS MVP structure and recommend the top 3 critical conversion events I should track to validate product-market fit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Identify and document the core conversion events for my MVP based on the current codebase.'

steps.title: Bootstrap Analytics Provider
steps.description: Initialize a lightweight, high-performance analytics SDK that doesn't bloat your bundle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular analytics service wrapper in TypeScript that supports segmenting by user ID and event properties.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Create src/services/analytics.ts and integrate the recommended SDK with environment variable protection.'

steps.title: Instrument Critical User Journeys
steps.description: Place tracking hooks at the exact points where users make value-based decisions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Find the signup and checkout components in my codebase and inject trackEvent calls for 'user_signed_up' and 'subscription_started'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Scan for signup/checkout components and add analytics tracking hooks to all interaction points.'

steps.title: Implement Session Replay for Context
steps.description: Numbers tell you the 'what,' but replays tell you the 'why.' Add visual debugging to your stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a session recording provider to capture user interactions, ensuring PII masking is enabled by default.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Install and configure session recording SDK in the main application entry point.'

steps.title: Automate Reporting Alerts
steps.description: Don't check dashboards; let the data come to you when anomalies occur.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script that queries my analytics API and sends a summary report to my Slack webhook every 24 hours.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a cron job script to fetch daily conversion metrics and post them to the configured Slack webhook.'

pro_tips: Track 'Feature Intent' clicks before you even build the feature to gauge real demand.
Always use a centralized event schema to prevent 'data rot' as your codebase scales.
Never send PII (names, emails) to third-party analytics; map everything to anonymous UUIDs.
Focus on cohort retention rates rather than total signups; growth without retention is a leaky bucket.
Use Claude Code to audit your analytics implementation monthly to ensure no events were broken by refactoring.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, force yourself to spend 20 minutes a day analyzing user flows. Use those insights to kill features that aren't used and double down on the ones that drive revenue.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark. Your MVP deserves to be measured, optimized, and scaled by someone who understands the business of code. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
